title: Quercus × yokohamensis
taxonomy: Quercus × yokohamensis is a hybrid oak tree species, resulting from the cross between Quercus acutissima and Quercus serrata. It is native to East Asia. As a hybrid, its classification can be complex. The exact classification of Quercus × yokohamensis may vary depending on the source. It is often classified as a hybrid species within the Quercus genus.

properties: Quercus × yokohamensis has been studied for its potential therapeutic properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. parts: Quercus × yokohamensis, also known as the Yokohama oak, is a deciduous tree species native to Japan and China. The bark, leaves, and acorns of Quercus × yokohamensis are used in traditional medicine in these regions. The bark is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating various ailments. The leaves are also used to treat fever, and the acorns are used to treat digestive issues. However, the efficacy and safety of these medicinal parts require further scientific evaluation.
preparations: Quercus × yokohamensis is known to have various applications in traditional herbal medicine, particularly in Japan. intro: Quercus × yokohamensis is a hybrid tree species, resulting from the cross between Japanese oak (Quercus acutissima) and Chinese evergreen oak (Quercus glauca), characterized by its large leaves and broad, rounded crown. The health benefits of Quercus × yokohamensis include its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to protect against chronic diseases such as heart disease, diabetes, and certain types of cancer. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and tannins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Quercus × yokohamensis is often used to make herbal preparations such as tea, extracts, and infusions, which are believed to promote cardiovascular health, improve respiratory function, and boost the immune system.
